title: thlaspi montanum subsp. lotharingum
intro: Thlaspi montanum subsp. lotharingum, also known as Alpine Pennycress or Thlaspi, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a perennial plant native to the mountains of Europe, characterized by its small white flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Alpine Pennycress can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or used in salads, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation, promoting digestion, and supporting immune function.
